pages_preview.text_preview: Monitor Recruitment Protocol NRG Metaketa III August 28, 2017 Preliminaries This document describes the principles for recruiting monitors as part of EGAP’s Metaketa III, Natural Resource Governance project. It is based on the premise that field conditions will make it impossible to fully harmonize how monitors are recruited and selected, but that each team should comply with common principles and procedures for recruitment. 1 Recruitment Procedure Monitors are recruited with the input of community members and/or leaders, following a detailed discussion of the goal of the monitoring program, the criteria for recruitment, and the concepts of community natural resource management. 2 Requirements Monitorsshouldbecommunitymemberswhoareidentifiedasavailabletoparticipateinmonitoring and exhibiting the leadership qualities that monitoring requires. Each committee is encouraged to have at least one woman. Committee members must have adequate literacy and numeracy to conduct and manage the monitoring and record keeping. 3 Selection Procedure The trainers will explain the monitoring protocol, the expectations for monitors over the duration of the project, and the compensation for monitors. The community and/or community leaders will identify three to six community monitors to form a “community resource committee” that will be responsible for resource monitoring. Themembersofthecommitteewillbeselectedeitherthroughself-nomination,recommendation by community members who are present at a full meeting workshop, or nominations from resource managers as long as the relevant community characteristics are taken into account. The trainers will seek to identify if there are community members who meet the criteria listed above. Nominated members will say whether or not they accept the position. If there are more than four people nominated, then the community members who are present will hold a vote (open or closed, depending on local norms). 1
